commit.message:
fix: auto-update deploys SKILL.md to projects, setup-project detects outdated content

- auto-update-tools.sh: add deploy_skills() step after pull — compares content
  hash and updates SKILL.md in project .github/skills/ and .claude/skills/
- setup-project.py: compare MD5 hash instead of just checking file existence,
  so outdated SKILL.md gets updated on re-run
- templates/SKILL.md: add new features (--wakeup, --titles-only, --wing/--room,
  --fact, --relate, 7 observation types, auto-update commands)

commit.message:
fix: CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID empty string now falls back to default sheet ID

os.environ.get() with a default only works when var is UNSET.
When bot.env contains 'CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID=' (empty value), Python returns
empty string, causing URL 'spreadsheets/d//edit#gid=0'. Fixed with 'or'.
Also documented CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID in bot.env.template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Yandex Station Player Provider for Music Assistant

commit.message:
Add randomize campaign feature - 'Surprise Me' button

Backend:
- POST /campaigns/randomize picks a random name from 15 evocative
  titles (The Shattered Crown, Ashes of the Old Gods, etc.) and a
  random tone (dark_fantasy / gritty / comedic / storybook)
- Calls narrator.generate_world() to generate a full AI world context
- Falls back to tone-matched world snippet if no API key is set
- Campaign is fully ready to play immediately

Frontend:
- New '🎲 Surprise Me' button next to 'New Campaign' on the home screen
- Purple/arcane styling to distinguish it from normal campaign creation
- Pulses with a glow animation while the world is being generated
- Navigates directly to the new campaign once created

CSS:
- .btn-randomize: purple gradient, glows on hover, pulse animation
  while disabled/loading
- .campaign-list-actions: flex container for button group

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.2.55: Fix timezone bug — resolve TZ from HA Supervisor API at startup

At startup, app now resolves timezone via:
1. TZ env var
2. /data/options.json 'timezone' option
3. HA Core /api/config time_zone via Supervisor API
Applies os.environ['TZ'] + time.tzset() so date.today() uses local time.
Added 'timezone' option to config.json schema for manual override.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: HA-chores
repository.description: Gamified household chore management for Home Assistant

commit.message:
Fix worker spawning: process.execPath is copilot.exe, not node

process.execPath in the Copilot CLI runtime points to copilot.exe (a
standalone PE binary), not a Node.js binary. Spawning workers with it
fails because copilot.exe doesn't understand -e or .mjs arguments.

Added extensions/lib/resolve-node.mjs with resolveNodeBinary() that:
1. Probes process.execPath to see if it actually is Node
2. Falls back to node/node.exe on PATH via where/which
3. Caches the result for subsequent calls
4. Throws a clear error if neither works

Updated both ado-pr-watcher and ado-build-watcher to use it.
Updated CI expected files and copilot instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add suggested specification docs (#58)

* Consolidate TeamsActivity-related types into single file per language

Merge TeamsActivityBuilder, TeamsChannelData, TeamsConversation, and related
types (TenantInfo, ChannelInfo, TeamInfo, MeetingInfo, NotificationInfo) into
the main TeamsActivity file in each language:

- .NET: TeamsActivity.cs now contains all Teams types
- Node: teams-activity.ts now contains all Teams types
- Python: teams_activity.py now contains all Teams types

Deleted redundant files:
- dotnet: TeamsActivityBuilder.cs, TeamsChannelData.cs, TeamsConversation.cs
- node: teams-activity-builder.ts, teams-channel-data.ts, teams-conversation.ts
- python: teams_activity_builder.py, teams_channel_data.py, teams_conversation.py

All tests pass across all three languages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add suggested specification docs

Create the six documentation files listed in the README as suggested
additional docs:

- specs/ActivityPayloads.md — Annotated JSON examples for each activity type
- specs/Configuration.md — Per-language configuration reference
- specs/Middleware.md — Middleware spec with patterns and cross-language ref
- specs/ProactiveMessaging.md — Proactive messaging via ConversationClient
- specs/Samples.md — Walkthrough of all samples with annotated code
- specs/Contributing.md — Behavioral invariants, CI, and new language porting

Update README.md to link to the new docs (replacing the suggested list)
and update specs/README.md to include the new docs in the spec table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: name portable artifact with version and commit hash (#12995)

Derive the GHA artifact name from the actual zip filename produced by
the CreatePortable MSBuild target, so the artifact appears in the GHA
UI as e.g. GitExtensions-Portable-x64-7.0.0.61-1b6954a rather than
the generic 'portable'.

Addresses feedback in #12983.

commit.message:
docs: add PlantUML class diagram of LSP server architecture

Comprehensive UML class diagram covering:
- Server core (lifecycle, document sync, diagnostics)
- 5 services (ValidationPipeline, CatalogValidation, RngValidation,
  KeySpace, SubjectScheme) with interfaces
- 13-phase validation pipeline with phase handlers
- 11 LSP language feature modules (completion, hover, definition, etc.)
- Utility modules (xmlTokenizer, textUtils, referenceParser, i18n)
- Data modules (ditaSchema, ditaSpecialization)
- All relationships: implements, owns, uses, wires

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Tournament mode — single-elimination bracket with 8/16 players, Firestore sync, champion badge and scoring (#84)

* feat: implement tournament mode single-elimination bracket

- Add domain models: TournamentPlayer, TournamentMatch, TournamentStatus, Tournament
- Add TournamentRepository interface
- Add CreateTournamentUseCase and AdvanceTournamentUseCase
- Add FirestoreTournamentRepository with Firestore sync
- Add TournamentViewModel, TournamentSetupScreen, TournamentBracketScreen
- Add tournament navigation routes and menu button
- Add unit tests for CreateTournamentUseCase and TournamentViewModel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: cancleeric <9006516+cancleeric@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove repositories block from core/build.gradle.kts

FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS is set in settings.gradle.kts dependencyResolutionManagement.
mavenCentral() is already declared there and applies to all modules including :core.
Adding a project-level repositories block causes a build failure.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: cancleeric <9006516+cancleeric@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Hurricane CTO <hurricane-cto@hurricanedigital.com>

commit.message:
Add top 10,000 canonical baseball card database

Comprehensive seed database covering 481 players across 368 set-years
from 17 manufacturers, spanning 1909 T206 to 2025 modern releases.

- Generator script (scripts/generate_top_cards.py) with full player
  database organized by era and importance tier (1-5)
- 151 hand-curated key cards with exact card numbers (T206 Wagner,
  1952 Topps Mantle #311, 1989 UD Griffey #1, etc.)
- 1,758 rookie cards identified and flagged
- Covers all major sets: Topps, Bowman, Fleer, Donruss, Upper Deck,
  Score, Topps Chrome, Bowman Chrome, SP, Finest, Heritage, Prizm
- Decade distribution: 1900s-1940s (299), 1950s-70s (1,406),
  1980s-90s (3,864), 2000s-20s (4,444)
- Seed function skips duplicates (39 iconic cards already existed)
- All 118 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
